blocks.content: A brown liquor bottle shattered against the castle wall.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Come out, you bastards! Fight me!
Drunk, Jokre shouted at the top of his lungs. His pupils were completely dilated, and he staggered in a zigzag pattern.
Rudvans and Vivian supported him from either side.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Jokre, stop it. What if we get caught? We're not supposed to be here at this hour.
His gloomy mood had turned to anger through the alcohol.
Jokre needed someone to fight, and his defiance against the world led him to trespass into the restricted area.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Haha! Who's going to stop me? I'm a mage! An official mage! Bring it on! I'll blow away any guards with a single spell!
Jokre shook off his friends and stumbled forward.
Rudvans and Vivian couldn't stop him. They were also drunk and barely coherent.
Rudvans, who was the most sober, grabbed Jokre again and led him into the castle.

blocks.speaker: Rudvans
blocks.content: It's cold. Let's go inside. If you keep shouting, we'll be in big trouble.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Drinks! Rudvans! Let's drink! Let's drink to our hearts' content!

blocks.speaker: Rudvans
blocks.content: Alright, just be quiet and follow me.
The hall, now empty of people, felt eerie.
Objects they had passed by indifferently during the day now seemed to emit a creepy aura, as if they had come to life.
The Ice Queen's figure, illuminated by the cold moonlight through the window, was especially chilling.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Can we get some light magic?
Jokre was too drunk to cast a spell, so Rudvans and Vivian attempted the light magic.
After several failed attempts due to their inebriation, they finally succeeded.
With a pop, two lights floated to the ceiling, instantly brightening the hall.
Jokre crawled up the slippery ice stairs.
Reaching the 1.5th-floor corridor, he picked a fight with the wax figure of the Ice Queen encased in ice.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Hey, Ice Queen? Are you asleep? Want to have a drink with me?
There was no response.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Haha! See? Even legendary mages are nothing special. Just stuck in ice, sleeping! Is that even magic?
Magic. Law that twists phenomena. Ice Queen. Mage.

blocks.speaker: Optros
blocks.content: The mental entity absorbed Jokre's information.
Materialized by Lyria's necromantic summoning, Optros instinctively fled to the place where the most familiar thoughts were concentrated.
That place was the Ice Queen's castle.
Among the information spewed by tourists, the Ice Queen held the most significance.
Optros quickly adapted to this world based on the vast amount of information.
At some point, the mental entity, which had only existed as a sense, developed cognition.
As sense and cognition communicated, the preservation of simple knowledge began to transition into the stage of thought.

blocks.speaker: Optros
blocks.content: I think, therefore I am.

blocks.speaker: Optros
blocks.content: Optros concluded that it needed a form to prove its existence. It sought a special vessel to materialize its thoughts.
The Ice Queen's eyes snapped open.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Jokre wasn't surprised. He knew that stepping on the wax figure's pedestal would make its eyes open.
The Ice Queen's beautiful appearance made his wounds, carelessly inflicted by a woman, throb even more.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Jokre knocked on the ice as if knocking on a door.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Hey, Queen. Want to date me? Hahaha.
Vivian looked at Jokre's drunken antics with disdain and clicked her tongue.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: He's completely lost it. Hey, get a grip. If you pass out here, you'll freeze to death.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Alright, let's do this. Mage versus mage. If I win, you date me.
The information Optros had gathered was categorized as experience, and it reached the stage of judging the world by its own standards.
What is strength? Superior traits. Control of properties.
Alteration, transformation, change of the physical world.
Then a question arose.
Then... am I strong?

blocks.speaker: Rudvans
blocks.content: Enough already. Let's go back. We need to sleep. How long are you going to keep this up?
Seeing Jokre's half-closed eyes, Rudvans grabbed him from behind and dragged him away.
Vivian, who had been sighing with her arms crossed, glanced at the Ice Queen and then followed Rudvans.
But as she took a step, she turned her head sharply in shock.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: W-what?
The Ice Queen's eyes, which should have been looking straight ahead, were now staring at her.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Vivian thought she was still drunk.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: But no matter how much she tried to clear her head, the Ice Queen's gaze was unmistakably turned sideways.
It felt like her hair was standing on end. Her heart pounded as if it would burst, and she was in shock.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Guys, over here...
Jokre's voice faded down the stairs.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Date me... huh?

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Don't leave me!

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Vivian was too terrified to scream.
The Ice Queen's wax eyes, which were clearly made of wax, now moved freely, scanning the surroundings.
It seemed to be analyzing the material that confined it.
Vivian felt like she was going to die, even though she wasn't in pain.
No, it was scarier than dying.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: As she felt the distance from her friends growing, her desire to live filled her lungs with air.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: We'll do anything you ask. Just please spare our lives.
The Ice Queen asked with genuine curiosity.
She had taken on a human form and met humans. She was curious about what they would do to her.
Pure curiosity and madness often go hand in hand, and the Ice Queen's honest question filled Jokre with fear.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: We'll do anything you ask. Just please spare our lives.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Anything I ask?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Yes, anything...
The Ice Queen tilted her head.
Why?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Of course, Your Majesty is a ruler. A great mage. So you should rule again.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Rule?
The Ice Queen tilted her head further, then frowned as if something displeased her.
Though she had gained individuality by taking on a human form, her information processing speed was significantly slower.
But she accepted this neural discrepancy as a characteristic of living beings, not as something unpleasant.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Absolutely right. We are nothing compared to you.
Jokre groveled endlessly.
He had worked his whole life to achieve his first success.
If he died a meaningless death here before enjoying life as a mage, he wouldn't be able to rest in peace even as a corpse.
